description:
## Goal

Extract the chunked-with-shutdown-check sleep primitive that is
currently duplicated in two places:
- `src/fetcher/refresh_supervisor.cr` (private instance method
  on `Supervisor`)
- `src/fetcher/refresh_health_reporter.cr` (private class
  method on `HealthReporter`)

into a single `RefreshLoop::InterruptibleSleep` helper. Both
consumers call into it.

## Why now

- The duplication is documented in `quickhea-az6.3` as a
  deliberate stop-gap; that ticket said "if a shared sleep
  utility is extracted later, this can be replaced." This is
  that "later".
- The chunked-shutdown-check is the kind of primitive that
  benefits most from a spec.
- A spec already exists for `SemaphorePool` (the most
  refactor-prone neighbor) — the sleep helper is the next
  logical thing to lock down with a spec.

## Design

`src/fetcher/interruptible_sleep.cr`:

```crystal
require "../module"

module RefreshLoop
  module InterruptibleSleep
    DEFAULT_CHUNK = 30.seconds

    def self.sleep(total : Time::Span, outer_cap : Time::Span? = nil, chunk : Time::Span = DEFAULT_CHUNK) : Time::Span
      cap = outer_cap || total
      elapsed = Time::Span.zero
      while elapsed < total && elapsed < cap && !QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?
        step = {chunk, total - elapsed, cap - elapsed}.min
        select
        when timeout(step)
          elapsed += step
        end
      end
      elapsed
    end
  end
end
```

Notes:
- The `Supervisor` version takes `outer_cap` and `chunk` and
  returns elapsed; the `HealthReporter` version takes no
  params and returns Nil. The unified API returns the
  elapsed Time::Span (callers that want Nil can ignore it).
- `DEFAULT_CHUNK` is the single source of truth; the
  `DEFAULT_SLEEP_CHUNK` constant in `Supervisor` and the
  `SHUTDOWN_CHUNK` constant in `HealthReporter` can both
  become aliases (or be removed entirely in favor of the
  shared one).

## Refactor

- `Supervisor`: remove the private `#interruptible_sleep`
  instance method. The two call sites
  (`run_first_cycle`, `sleep_between_cycles`,
  `handle_outer_error`) call
  `RefreshLoop::InterruptibleSleep.sleep(...)`. The
  `DEFAULT_SLEEP_CHUNK` class constant is removed
  (delegated to `InterruptibleSleep::DEFAULT_CHUNK`).
- `HealthReporter`: remove the private `#interruptible_sleep`
  class method. The call site in `#start` calls
  `RefreshLoop::InterruptibleSleep.sleep(REPORT_INTERVAL)`.
  The `SHUTDOWN_CHUNK` class constant is removed.

## Spec

`spec/interruptible_sleep_spec.cr`:
- sleep for 0 seconds returns immediately with elapsed = 0
- sleep for a short duration returns elapsed close to total
- sleep is interrupted by `QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?`
  returning true
- `outer_cap` caps the sleep below `total` even without
  shutdown
- `chunk` controls the granularity of the internal timeout
  (a very small chunk means many iterations; not strictly
  observable from outside but the timing should be close to
  total for a chunk of 100ms and a sleep of 200ms)

## Acceptance

- `src/fetcher/interruptible_sleep.cr` exists with the new
  module
- `src/fetcher/refresh_supervisor.cr` no longer has the
  private `#interruptible_sleep` method
- `src/fetcher/refresh_health_reporter.cr` no longer has the
  private `#interruptible_sleep` method
- `spec/interruptible_sleep_spec.cr` exists and passes
- Full suite (228+ examples) still passes
- `ameba` clean
- `just nix-build` succeeds
- A boot smoke test confirms the supervisor and the health
  reporter both still work end-to-end (they share the
  sleep path, so this is the most important regression test)
title: Extract RefreshLoop::InterruptibleSleep and refactor both consumers
